name: Variable Jump Height
description: A jump model where release timing changes apex height by cutting upward velocity, changing gravity, or limiting sustained impulse.
design_notes: Variable jump height needs deterministic input timing. Define jump_input_timestamp_ms, jump hold interval, release cutoff velocity, gravity multipliers, coyote time and jump buffer interaction, and how network or replay systems record the input. The mechanic should let players make short hops and full jumps intentionally rather than guessing at hidden thresholds.
design_purpose: Give players expressive vertical control for short hops, full jumps, and precise landings.
Support level designs with varied ceiling heights, enemy stomps, ledges, and platform spacing.
Make platforming feel responsive by tying jump result to readable input timing.
player_fantasy: The player feels precise and agile, shaping the jump arc through intentional button timing.

parameters.name: jump_hold_window_ms
parameters.description: Maximum held-input interval that can extend jump height.

parameters.name: jump_release_cutoff_velocity
parameters.description: Upward velocity clamp applied when jump input is released early.

parameters.name: apex_gravity_multiplier
parameters.description: Gravity multiplier near jump apex for feel and readability.

parameters.name: fall_gravity_multiplier
parameters.description: Gravity multiplier applied after upward travel ends.

parameters.name: jump_buffer_ms
parameters.description: Queued input interval accepted before the character can jump.

parameters.name: post_ground_jump_grace_ms
parameters.description: Post-ground grace interval that still permits jump start.

edge_cases: Jump release can arrive before the physics tick that consumes buffered jump input.
Coyote time and jump buffering can overlap and must not create two jumps from one press.
Head collision during held jump should cut upward velocity and clear held-jump extension.
Replay and rollback systems must record press and release timestamps, not just final jump height.
common_bugs: Jump release is read in render update and produces different arcs by frame rate.
Holding jump through a landing immediately triggers another full-height jump unintentionally.
Apex gravity multiplier stacks with low-gravity zones and creates floaty or infinite arcs.
Animation events fire jump state changes after physics already resolved the arc.
balancing_notes: Wide hold windows are forgiving but can make short hops harder unless release cutoff is strong.
Fast fall multipliers make jumps snappy but reduce reaction time for landing corrections.
Level spacing should be tested with both minimum and maximum jump arcs, not only full-height jumps.
accessibility_notes: Offer input buffering and optional wider jump hold windows for precision-heavy games.
Avoid requiring extremely short taps for required routes unless assist settings or alternate paths exist.

implementation_notes.unity: Record jump press and release timestamps before FixedUpdate, then resolve coyote_time_ms and jump_buffer_ms in the jump state machine. Apply velocity cutoff and gravity multipliers in physics code, with gizmos showing accepted windows and arcs.
implementation_notes.godot: Store press/release ticks on CharacterBody and update vertical velocity in _physics_process. Use debug drawing to show coyote, buffer, and cutoff states when tuning platforming levels.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use CharacterMovement jump state plus explicit input timestamp tracking for press and release. Networked play should replicate or predict input sequence data so rollback produces the same jump arc.
implementation_notes.web: Keep jump state in the fixed simulation loop with input timestamps, hold window, and gravity multipliers. Rendered animation follows committed physics state rather than deciding jump height.

example_games.title: Super Mario Bros.
example_games.note: Uses variable jump height as a core platforming control.

example_games.title: Celeste
example_games.note: Uses responsive jump control alongside other precision assists.
